name: base_need_per_1000
description: Underlying monthly primary-care-relevant need per 1,000 population before access constraints.
real_data_needed: Monthly encounters, unmet need, population denominators
estimation_strategy: Estimate from encounter data plus unmet-need surveys by locality
tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: urgent_need_share
description: Share of primary-care-relevant need that is urgent/same-day or after-hours sensitive.
real_data_needed: Booking urgency, after-hours encounters, ED substitutable care
estimation_strategy: Multinomial demand model by urgency and patient group
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: routine_need_share
description: Share of need that is routine or planned rather than urgent.
tier_rationale: Useful for political economy, narrative, risk, or implementation analysis; should not drive headline conclusions without validation.

name: chronic_need_index
description: Relative chronic and multimorbidity demand load.
source_or_basis: Capitation reweighting identifies multimorbidity as relevant
real_data_needed: Multimorbidity counts, LTC registers, medication/diagnosis proxies
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: rurality_demand_modifier
description: Additional access load or complexity caused by rurality and travel constraints.
real_data_needed: Rurality classification, travel time, provider proximity
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: deprivation_demand_modifier
description: Additional need/access burden related to socioeconomic deprivation.
real_data_needed: NZDep, unmet need, ED and admission outcomes
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: multimorbidity_demand_modifier
description: Extra contact intensity required for multimorbidity beyond average need.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: price_elasticity_general
description: How strongly general patients reduce primary care use when co-payments increase.
real_data_needed: Practice fees, CSC/VLCA status, utilisation changes
tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: price_elasticity_high_need
description: How strongly high-need groups reduce or delay care when co-payments increase.
source_or_basis: NZ Health Survey reports cost barriers by group
tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: telehealth_acceptability
description: Share of demand that can realistically be met by telehealth without undermining care quality.
tier_rationale: Useful for political economy, narrative, risk, or implementation analysis; should not drive headline conclusions without validation.

name: unmet_need_persistence
description: Persistence of unmet need from one month to the next.
real_data_needed: Delayed care, repeat attempts, ED conversion
tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: delay_complexity_growth
description: Rate at which delayed care becomes more complex or costly.
tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: gp_capacity_index
description: General practitioner capacity relative to population need.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: nurse_np_capacity_index
description: Nurse and nurse practitioner capacity available for claimable primary activity.
real_data_needed: NP/nurse FTE and scope of activity
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: pharmacist_capacity_index
description: Pharmacist capacity for eligible primary medical or protocolised contacts.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: allied_health_capacity_index
description: Allied health capacity for eligible musculoskeletal, mental health or chronic-care contacts.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: paramedic_alt_capacity_index
description: Paramedic or extended-care paramedic capacity for alternative disposition/treat-and-refer pathways.
source_or_basis: Needs ambulance workforce and pathway data
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: medical_productivity_per_fte
description: Relative monthly contacts generated per medical FTE under current settings.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: np_nurse_productivity_per_fte
description: Relative contacts generated per nurse/NP FTE when claimable and governed.
tier_rationale: Important for design, distribution, or implementation, but not alone decisive for the central claim.

name: scope_substitution_rate
description: Proportion of GP-bottlenecked contacts that can be safely shifted to other providers within scope.

tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: workforce_exit_rate
description: Monthly risk of provider/practice capacity exit under financial/workload stress.

name: market_entry_response
description: Provider/practice entry or expansion response to clear activity-sensitive payment rules.

tier_rationale: Directly affects the main thesis: whether upstream primary/urgent/ambulance supply expands safely or pressure shifts to hospitals.

name: local_inperson_constraint
description: Constraint on local in-person capacity, especially rural or under-served settings.

name: rural_loading_response

name: capitation_base_strength

name: scheduled_medical_benefit_strength

name: scheduled_benefit_price_adequacy

name: activity_signal_strength

name: patient_copayment_level

name: acc_activity_strength

name: acc_constraint_intensity

name: pho_transaction_cost

name: place_based_accountability_strength

name: global_cap_constraint

name: data_observability_primary

name: data_observability_ambulance

name: hospital_salience

name: equity_program_strength

name: hospital_cost_per_event_index

name: cherry_picking_risk

name: low_value_activity_risk
